--- SOURCE 1: Precision livestock farming workshops - Pertecnica Engineering ---
URL: https://www.pertecnica.net/precision-livestock-farming-workshops/

SUMMARY:
Pertecnica Engineering's Precision Livestock Farming Workshops are designed to provide hands-on training and insights into the latest technologies and methods

FULL CONTENT:
#702, 7th Floor, Dega Towers, Rajbhavan Road, Somajiguda, Hyderabad - 500082

![Pertecnica Engineering](https://www.pertecnica.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/pertecnica-e1751627177457.png)

# Precision Livestock Farming Workshops

#### Overview

Precision Livestock Farming (PLF) leverages advanced technologies and data analytics to optimize livestock management, enhance animal health, and improve productivity. Pertecnica Engineering’s Precision Livestock Farming Workshops are designed to provide hands-on training and insights into the latest technologies and methods in precision livestock management. Our workshops equip professionals with the skills needed to implement precision techniques and drive efficiency in livestock operations.

#### Workshop Modules

**1. Introduction to Precision Livestock Farming**

**2. Data Collection and Analysis**

**3. Health and Welfare Monitoring**

**4. Feeding and Nutrition Management**

**5. Reproduction and Breeding Optimization**

**6. Environmental Impact and Resource Efficiency**

**7. Animal Behavior and Performance Monitoring**

**8. Precision Livestock Farming Technologies**

**9. Case Studies and Best Practices**

**10. Future Trends and Innovations**

**11. Implementation and Integration**

**12. Data Privacy and Security**

#### Who Should Attend

Our Precision Livestock Farming Workshops are ideal for:

--- SOURCE 4: 2025usplf-lincoln ---
URL: https://enreec.unl.edu/2025usplf-lincoln/

SUMMARY:
The 2025 USPLF conference is the 3rd on Precision Livestock Farming, bringing together professionals to apply technology to improve animal care and well-being.

FULL CONTENT:
## Search Form

# 2025usplf-lincoln

![2025 USPLF-Lincoln](/sites/unl.edu.ianr.extension.enreec/files/styles/no_crop_960/public/media/image/2025PrecisionAgConferenceHeader.png?itok=ayWRQQsR)
![2025 USPLF-Lincoln Save the Date](/sites/unl.edu.ianr.extension.enreec/files/styles/no_crop_960/public/media/image/SAVE_THE_DATE_NO_CODE.png?itok=2cnHpDgE)

#### **The 3rd US Conference on Precision Livestock Farming**

The USPLF conference aims to bring together scientists, engineers, veterinarians, companies, farmers, policymakers, and associations who are interested in applying technology to improve animal care and well-being.

At this conference, we are building a platform for professionals to engage and exchange ideas within the entire PLF community. Our goal is to highlight state-of-the-art research and groundbreaking innovations while critically evaluating the overall sustainability of our animal systems. As a truly transdisciplinary event, the conference provides an ideal place to share research results and gain inspiration for the future

**PLF (Precision Livestock Farming)**

Precision Livestock Farming involves applying technology directly to animal environments to monitor health, welfare, and production while reducing environmental impact. At its core, PLF uses tools that aid in animal care and inform management decisions. These tools have also helped design modern animal facilities and create phenotypic data for better genetic selection.  Moreover, Precision Livestock Farming can be applied to a wide range of animals, including insects, fish, companion animals, and zoo animals.

#### **About the University of Nebraska-Lincoln**

The University of Nebraska–Lincoln was chartered as a land-grant university on February 15, 1869, to create opportunity for the state of Nebraska. A proud member of the [Big Ten Conference](http://www.bigten.org/), the [Big Ten Academic Alliance](https://www.btaa.org/home), and the [Association of Public and Land-grant Universities (APLU)](http://www.aplu.org/), Nebraska is classified within the Carnegie “[R1: Doctoral Universities – Highest Research Activity](http://carnegieclassifications.iu.edu/)” category. The university is accredited by the [Higher Learning Commission](https://www.hlcommission.org/).

Learn about the University of Nebraska–Lincoln:  [https://unl.edu](https://unl.edu/)

--- SOURCE 5: Farming Operations Technology - TCAT Jackson ---
URL: https://tcatjackson.edu/programs/farming-operations-technology

SUMMARY:
Students may earn certificates by completing courses that offers exposure to principles in agriculture, livestock equipment, and precision agriculture. Other

FULL CONTENT:
[Jump to navigation](#main-menu)
[Skip to Content](#main-content)

![Tennessee College of Applied Technology - Jackson](/profiles/tcat/themes/tcat_theme/images/logos/logo-jackson.svg)

## Search form

# Farming Operations Technology

## About

This program will provide students with skills and knowledge needed for today’s farming operations which include, but not limited to, row crops, animal agriculture, and precision agriculture.  Students may earn certificates by completing courses that offers exposure to principles in agriculture, livestock equipment, and precision agriculture.  Other courses for credentials will present welding, farm based electricity, and shop principles as well as agriculture finance.  Students will be presented material for understanding farm machinery maintenance, soil and plant management, crop and livestock marketing, and herd management.  Precision agriculture is the future of the industry and will be offered as an individual diploma.

## Overview

| Information | Detail |
| --- | --- |
| Time Commitment | Full Time |

| Clock Hours | 1,728 |
| Class Type | Day |
| Credentials | Certificate, Diploma |
| Total Tuition/Fees | $5,760.00 |
| Textbook/Supplies Cost | $1,599.49 |

enriched_points.expanded_content: Training programs focus on teaching farmers the latest precision livestock farming technologies, enhancing their capability to utilize data-driven insights for improved livestock management.
Hands-on workshops, like those offered by Pertecnica Engineering, provide essential resources, case studies, and best practices in precision livestock farming to facilitate practical learning.
Courses and seminars help farmers develop technical proficiencies in data collection, health monitoring, and feeding management, ensuring they can effectively implement precision farming techniques.
